top_signals.reason: Boeing holds two NASA contracts totaling over $32.9B, both exceeding $1B threshold (+15 each) and confirmed cross-referenced with 31 corruption indicators and social mentions (+25). Combined signal with dual contract stacking produces a high-conviction institutional accumulation setup.

Howard Lutnick confirmed in Paradise Papers (Malta registry) with 100% match score, triggering +30pts offshore confirmed signal. Cabinet-level figure with offshore entity confirmed raises conflict-of-interest and regulatory risk for associated holdings.

Boozman purchased two Treasury bond ETFs (IEF and IEI) on the same date, suggesting a deliberate rate or macro call with multiple same-ticker-cluster signals triggering x1.3 multiplier. Flight-to-safety positioning by a Senate member may signal advance knowledge of economic or policy stress.

Same-day paired Treasury ETF purchase alongside IEF amplifies signal via multiple-trade clustering and x1.3 multiplier. Coordinated bond positioning by a Senate appropriator warrants elevated attention.

Trump Jr posted about an Idaho chip maker reaching $1T valuation fastest ever, signaling insider awareness of semiconductor momentum (+10pts data center/AI infrastructure). Multiple AI-related posts on same day suggest coordinated narrative building around chip sector.

Military construction appropriations bill HR8469 passed House and received in Senate, indicating sustained defense spending through FY2027. Defense sector ETFs and military construction contractors positioned to benefit from continued appropriations flow.
Rex Tillerson confirmed in Paradise Papers (Appleby) at 100% match score (+30pts), but no current cabinet role or active trade signal reduces actionability. Signal noted for monitoring only.
Senate sale of eToro near IPO lock-up period warrants attention at meaningful size. No corroborating signals to amplify score beyond base congressional trade value.
Small purchase of a regional bank by a Senate member with no corroborating signals or major contract cross-reference. Low conviction signal at minimal dollar amount.
Senate purchase of JPMorgan is a small routine position with no supporting contract or cross-reference data. Insufficient signal strength to act upon.
Moderate Senate purchase of PepsiCo with no corroborating contract awards or cross-reference indicators. Appears routine with no elevated corruption or insider signal.
Small sale of S&P 500 Growth ETF with no corroborating signals. Minimal dollar value and no cross-reference indicators keep this at base score.
Small purchase of Eaton Corp near the cluster of Boozman bond trades but minimal amount and no direct contract cross-reference. Insufficient standalone signal.

Bechtel is privately held so no tradeable ticker exists; contract over $1B earns base points but no actionable equity signal can be generated. Flagged for private market or supplier chain awareness only.
